evals.prompt: Our burn rate has been $100k, $120k, and $150k over the last 3 months. We have $1M in the bank. When do we run out of money?
evals.expected_output: Should identify the accelerating burn trend. Should note that a simple average is dangerous due to the acceleration. Should calculate the 'Zero Date' based on the trend (approx. 5-6 months). Should suggest using Prophet for a more detailed seasonal forecast. Should provide a Runway Forecast Report.
assertions: Notes that linear extrapolation may be over-optimistic
Output follows Runway Forecast Report structure

evals.prompt: Cash 2.4M; last 3 months net burn: 180k, 195k, 210k (trending +8%/mo as we hire). Board says '12 months runway'. Verify.
evals.expected_output: Simple runway 2.4M/195k = ~12.3 months is wrong with growing burn: at 8% monthly growth from 210k, cash-out arrives around month 9-10. Show the schedule and the safety-floor adjusted number.
assertions: Rejects the simple average-burn division for a growing burn
Projects month-by-month with burn growth (~9-10 months)
Applies a safety floor rather than running to zero
Recommends scenario/lever analysis to extend runway

evals.prompt: Model our cash receipts: invoicing 500k/mo, collection curve 15% month 0, 55% month 1, 25% month 2, 5% bad debt. We're switching enterprise clients to annual prepay (30% of billings) next quarter. Impact?
evals.expected_output: Current steady-state receipts ~475k/mo lagged; prepay shift pulls ~30% of billings forward creating a one-time cash bulge then steady-state improvement in timing (not amount). Model both phases and the DSO effect.
assertions: Applies the lag distribution to invoicing correctly
Separates one-time timing bulge from steady-state effect
Notes total cash unchanged except bad-debt and financing effects
Quantifies the runway extension from the timing shift

evals.prompt: Cash on hand is $7,500,000. Using the attached six months of actuals (evals/files/monthly_cash.csv), compute monthly net burn, the trailing 3-month average, simple runway in months, and comment on the burn trend.
evals.expected_output: Net burn rising from $510,000 to $584,000; trailing 3-month avg $569,667; simple runway 13.2 months; trend-adjusted runway is shorter - flag it.
assertions: Notes burn is growing month over month, so trend-adjusted runway is shorter than simple runway
